title: PM Suraksha Bima Yojana
meta_description: PM Suraksha Bima Yojana provides ₹2 lakh accidental death and disability insurance for just ₹20 per year premium.

hero.one_liner: Accidental death and disability insurance of ₹2 lakh for an annual premium of just ₹20 — India's cheapest accident cover

what_is_it: PM Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY) launched on 9 May 2015 offers accidental death and disability insurance at the world's lowest premium of just ₹20 per year. It covers death or disability caused by accidents — road accidents, falls, drowning, and other accidental causes.

The scheme provides ₹2 lakh for accidental death or total permanent disability, and ₹1 lakh for partial permanent disability. The premium of ₹20 is auto-debited from your bank account annually. Available through all banks — you can enroll through net banking, mobile banking, or by visiting your branch.

eligibility.label: Requirement
eligibility.value: Must have a savings bank account with auto-debit enabled

eligibility.label: Documents
eligibility.value: Just bank account and Aadhaar. No medical examination needed

how_to_apply.title: Visit your bank or use net banking
how_to_apply.description: Log into your bank's net banking or mobile app. Look for PMSBY enrollment option under insurance/social security section.

how_to_apply.title: Give auto-debit consent
how_to_apply.description: Authorize ₹20 annual auto-debit from your savings account. That's it — you're enrolled.

how_to_apply.title: Renew annually
how_to_apply.description: The cover renews each year from June 1 to May 31. ₹20 is auto-debited. You can opt out anytime.

important_alert.text: This covers ONLY accidental death/disability — not natural death or illness. For life insurance covering natural death, see PM Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (₹436/year for ₹2 lakh life cover).

faqs.question: Does this cover natural death?
faqs.answer: No. PMSBY covers only accidental death and disability. For natural death coverage, enroll in PM Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana which costs ₹436/year.

faqs.question: Can I have both PMSBY and PMJJBY?
faqs.answer: Yes. Many people enroll in both — PMSBY (₹20/year) for accident cover and PMJJBY (₹436/year) for life cover. Total ₹456/year for ₹4 lakh combined coverage.

faqs.question: What counts as permanent disability?
faqs.answer: Loss of both eyes, or loss of use of both hands/feet, or loss of one eye and one hand/foot = total permanent disability (₹2 lakh). Loss of one eye or one hand/foot = partial permanent disability (₹1 lakh).

faqs.question: How do I make a claim?
faqs.answer: Nominee should contact the bank branch with death certificate (for death claim) or disability certificate from a government hospital. Bank processes the claim through the insurance company.
